title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · Linux Operations Room
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a distribution-aware package change record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Host proposes lab-tool 4.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 4.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
